title: sidalcea nitrophila
intro: Sidalcea nitrophila, also known as seep-spring checkermallow, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. Native to the northwestern United States and western Canada, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sidalcea nitrophila include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions and wounds. The herbal preparations of Sidalcea nitrophila include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
